name: When Does In-Context Search Help? A Sampling-Complexity Theory of Reflection-Driven Reasoning (2607.06720)
content: Training large language models (LLMs) with extended reasoning has enabled in-context search, in which models iteratively generate, critique, and revise solution attempts. We provide a theoretical analysis of in-context search by modeling it as approximate inference over reasoning traces, where the base model defines a prior and self-reflection provides feedback for posterior updates, and study the resulting inference-time sampling complexity - the number of sequential attempts needed to achieve
summary: 该研究通过理论分析探讨了在上下文搜索中引入自我反思的效果，发现当自我反思能够有效定位早期错误时，上下文搜索可以显著提升模型解决问题的能力。
key_insights: 上下文搜索能显著提高模型的解决能力
自我反思对于早期错误的定位至关重要
学习和训练方法对效果有重要影响
跨熵训练可恢复所需的行为
强化学习在多阶段抽象下具有相似机制
